title: Break-even Analysis: If a company’s total fixed costs are 120000 and variable...
summary: Calculate the break-even point in units by dividing fixed costs by the contribution margin per unit.
prompt: If a company’s total fixed costs are $120,000 and variable costs per unit are $15 with a selling price of $30 per unit, how many units must be sold to break even?
solution: Break-even Point = Fixed Costs / (Selling Price - Variable Cost) = $120,000 / ($30 - $15) = 8,000 units. This is crucial for financial planning (Page 51).
